steps.title: Find what the passage directly states about Whitten's work across decades
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'any tool that a painter uses to create an artwork is capable of creating gestures.': The passage says nothing about tools in general or their capacity for gesture. This goes far beyond the text. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Whitten’s work from the 1960s exhibits many more gestures than his work from the 1970s does.': The passage explicitly states Whitten’s 1970s process was a "clear departure from the prevalence of gestures in his work from the 1960s," and the art historians describe the shift as "removing gesture." Both perspectives confirm 1960s work had far more gesture. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Whitten became less interested in exploring the role of gesture in his work as his career progressed.': The passage describes a stylistic shift, not a change in interest or motivation. Inferring diminished interest is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Whitten’s work from the 1960s is much more realistic than his work from the 1970s is.': Realism is never mentioned. The debate is about gesture, not representational accuracy. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: Both Whitten’s own framing (departing from the prevalence of gestures) and the art historians’ framing (removing gesture) point to the same fact: more gesture in the 1960s, less in the 1970s. That matches choice B — B is correct.
